L1_tasks.title: Peak Staffing Model
L1_tasks.description: Review your staffing levels against your transaction data for the past four weeks. Are you adequately staffed during peak hours (typically 7-10am and 12-2pm)? If ticket times exceed 5 minutes during peak you are either understaffed or your workflow is broken. Observe your team during the next morning rush and identify every point of delay or confusion.

failure_modes.fm_title: Morning rush and peak management failure
Ticket time and service speed failure

L1_tasks.title: Peak Queue Management
L1_tasks.description: Measure your peak queue times this week. Time how long customers wait from joining the queue to receiving their order during morning rush (7-10am) and lunchtime peak. If any customer waits more than 5 minutes to order or more than 8 minutes for a coffee something is wrong. Identify the bottleneck immediately — is it the till the espresso machine the food prep or the team capacity?

failure_modes.fm_title: Queue management and wait time failure

L1_tasks.title: Seating Turnover Strategy
L1_tasks.description: Observe your seating area during peak periods. Are customers struggling to find seats? Are tables occupied by single customers with laptops during your busiest lunch hour? Count your covers and compare to your transaction data — are you turning tables or are they static? If seating is a bottleneck during peak it is costing you revenue.

failure_modes.fm_title: Seating management and turnover failure

L1_tasks.title: Daily Prep List Protocol
L1_tasks.description: Review your daily operational discipline. Are prep lists being used? Is the waste log completed every day? Is the maintenance log up to date? Are daily targets communicated to the team? If any of these basic disciplines have lapsed recommit to them this week. Operational discipline is the foundation everything else is built on.

failure_modes.fm_title: Operational discipline and daily routine failure

L1_tasks.title: Daily Team Briefing Protocol
L1_tasks.description: Are you holding daily team briefings before each shift? If not start tomorrow. A 5-minute huddle covering the day's targets any specials or changes and any issues from yesterday transforms team alignment. Check whether you have a weekly team meeting — if not schedule one. Communication gaps create errors waste and frustration.

failure_modes.fm_title: Team communication and meeting failure

L1_tasks.title: Seating Layout Optimisation
L1_tasks.description: Assess your cafe atmosphere critically. Is the lighting appropriate for the time of day? Is the temperature comfortable? Is the music at the right volume and genre? Sit in every seating position and experience the cafe from the customer's perspective. Identify the three things that most detract from the atmosphere and prioritise fixing them.

failure_modes.fm_title: Seating layout and comfort failure

L2_tasks.title: Peak Staffing Model
L2_tasks.description: Create a data-driven staffing model showing the optimal number of team members for each hour based on historical transaction volumes. Design role assignments for peak periods — dedicated till barista food prep and runner positions. Develop a mise-en-place checklist ensuring everything is prepared before the rush begins. Implement pre-rush routines that start 30 minutes before peak.

L2_tasks.title: Seating Turnover Strategy
L2_tasks.description: Develop a seating strategy that balances dine-in comfort with turnover efficiency. Create different zones — quick coffee seats community tables laptop-friendly areas and group seating. Implement a table-clearing protocol that maintains flow without rushing customers. Consider a peak-period policy for laptop users. Optimise your outdoor seating area if you have one.

failure_modes.fm_title: Seating management and turnover failure

L3_tasks.title: Peak Queue Management

Your seating should be a competitive advantage not an afterthought.
